title: Consolea × bahamana
intro: Consolea × bahamana, also known as Bahama dragon fruit, is a rare and unique succulent plant that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Consolea × bahamana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Consolea × bahamana is a hybrid species within the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a cross between Consolea crassifolia and Cephalanthera. This succulent hybrid is native to the Bahamas and is often cultivated for its unique and ornamental features. The classification of Consolea × bahamana is a result of its natural hybridization in the wild. As a hybrid species, it does not have a formal binomial nomenclature like its parent species.

Additionally, Consolea × bahamana is used in some cultures to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea.
